title: Daily Market Digest: Geopolitical Tensions Weigh on Indices Amid Mixed Earnings
subtitle: May 11, 2026 Market Close

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Edge Lower as Mideast Tensions Persist; Oil Prices Climb
items.summary: U.S. equities saw a cautious start to the week, with major indices generally slipping as investors reacted to ongoing geopolitical developments between the U.S. and Iran, which fueled a rise in oil prices. Despite record highs for the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite at Friday's close, Monday's trading reflected a pullback.

items.headline: Moderna Soars on Vaccine News
items.summary: Shares of Moderna spiked after the biotech company announced it was already developing a hantavirus vaccine.

items.headline: Intel Jumps on Apple Chip Deal Report
items.summary: Intel gained following reports of a preliminary agreement with Apple to manufacture some of its device chips.

items.headline: Lumentum Added to Nasdaq Index
items.summary: Lumentum climbed after Nasdaq announced its addition to the index.

items.headline: W.W. Grainger Slides on Profit-Taking
items.summary: Industrial supply company W.W. Grainger saw a significant decline as traders appeared to lock in recent gains after the stock hit record highs last week.

items.headline: Ventas Falls After Price Target Cut
items.summary: Ventas shares fell following a price target cut by JPMorgan.

items.headline: Palantir Drops Amid Valuation Concerns
items.summary: Palantir Technologies experienced a premarket dip due to valuation concerns and profit-taking, despite reporting strong Q1 earnings on May 5.

items.headline: Inflation Data, Fed Transition Eyed This Week
items.summary: Investors are closely watching for key economic data releases this week, including April's Consumer Price Index (CPI) and Producer Price Index (PPI) reports, which are expected to show inflation pushing above 3.5% year-over-year. The end of Jerome Powell's term as Fed Chair on Friday, with Kevin Warsh expected to be confirmed as his successor, also remains a focal point.

items.headline: Q1 Earnings Season Winds Down with Strong Growth
items.summary: The first-quarter 2026 earnings season is nearing its conclusion, with S&P 500 companies showing robust performance. Analysts' consensus forecasts indicate an aggregate EPS growth of 18.0% year-over-year for Q1, with full-year estimates implying 24.0% growth for 2026. This week, major companies like Cisco ($CSCO), Alibaba ($BABA), and Applied Materials ($AMAT) are scheduled to report.

items.headline: Technology Maintains Momentum, Energy Bolstered by Oil Prices
items.summary: While the broader market faced headwinds, the technology sector continued to show resilience, supported by strong earnings outlooks and ongoing demand for AI infrastructure. Conversely, rising oil prices, driven by the U.S.-Iran conflict, provided a boost to the energy sector, which remains a strong performer in 2026.

items.headline: Consumer Sentiment Dips to Lowest Since June 2022
items.summary: The University of Michigan reported that its preliminary index for consumer sentiment in May came in at 48.2%, marking the lowest reading since June 2022 and falling below consensus estimates.
